stories.title: Houghton bans new data centers for a year after resident pushback
stories.dek: City council voted a temporary moratorium while it writes rules on power use, noise, and water. The pause gives Houghton County residents time to weigh whether large facilities fit a city already short on electricity. Similar steps are now under discussion in nearby townships.
stories.source_title: Houghton City Council approves temporary data center moratorium - Upper Michigan's Source

stories.title: Houghton moratorium ripples across Upper Peninsula townships
stories.dek: Mining Journal reports neighboring communities are now studying their own pauses after seeing Houghton's action. Residents fear one large project could lock up regional transmission capacity for years. The debate has moved from one city to a multi-township conversation about who controls growth.

stories.title: Portage drafts rules to keep data centers out of neighborhoods
stories.dek: New zoning language would block large facilities from most residential and agricultural zones. Kalamazoo residents fear noise, traffic, and strain on the local grid if the rules are weakened. The changes come after months of complaints about industrial creep into rural edges of the city.
stories.source_title: Read Portage's proposed zoning changes to restrict data centers in the community - nwitimes.com

stories.title: Rural township hits pause on data-center tax deal after backlash
stories.dek: A Michigan township delayed its vote on a major data-center tax abatement after dozens of residents packed the meeting to object. The project would consume large amounts of electricity and water while delivering limited local tax revenue for years. The postponement keeps the outcome uncertain for nearby homeowners facing possible rate hikes.
stories.source_title: 5 takeaways: Rural township postpones data center tax abatement decision amid backlash - MLive.com

stories.title: Michigan town sues over nonstop data-center noise in federal court
stories.dek: Residents of one Michigan community claim an AI data center’s constant hum has made normal life impossible. The lawsuit asks a federal judge to intervene after local officials failed to act. Homeowners within earshot stand to gain relief or face years of disrupted sleep.

stories.title: Eaton planners flag power and water risks from Amazon-linked site
stories.dek: Eaton County commissioners questioned a data-center project tied to Amazon land during a study session. Staff were told to draft the county’s first zoning rules for the use. Nearby residents face higher utility bills and strained water supplies if it proceeds.

stories.title: Saline Township Delays 12-Year Tax Cut for Oracle Data Center
stories.dek: Board postponed action on the 50% abatement after residents questioned the consent judgment and raised concerns over water, noise and local costs for the $43 billion project.

stories.title: Girard Township locks in tight new rules for any data center
stories.dek: Branch County officials approved strict setbacks, noise limits, and height caps that make large projects far harder to build. The vote gives residents near Coldwater clearer protection against sudden industrial development. Local leaders say the rules still allow smaller facilities if they meet the new standards.
stories.source_title: Girard Township adopts strict zoning regulations for data centers - WTVB

stories.title: State officials may decide Mason-area data center instead of locals
stories.dek: A proposal near Mason could bypass township review and go straight to state regulators. Ingham County residents fear losing input on power use and tax deals. The shift raises questions about local control over big industrial projects.

stories.title: York Township schedules hearing on data center moratorium
stories.dek: Residents will weigh a temporary ban after similar pauses passed in nearby Adrian and other towns. The hearing comes as Washtenaw County sees rising opposition to large server farms. People want time to assess impacts on roads, water and electricity rates.

stories.title: Hundreds pack hearing as Garfield Township pushes data-center moratorium
stories.dek: Grand Traverse County planners recommended a temporary ban after residents filled the room to protest power lines and industrial noise near homes and farms. The proposed rules would give the township time to study water and electric-grid limits before any large project moves forward. Locals 30 minutes away are tracking the vote because any approved campus could affect regional electric rates.

national.title: Data centers advance rapidly as regulators scramble for rules
national.dek: Across the U.S., officials race to impose guardrails on power-hungry facilities while communities push back on incentives and impacts.

meetings.topic: Open meeting — oversees large-load electric agreements
